provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Liquor Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment of Liquor Control Authorities
provisions.title: Establishment of liquor control authority

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment of Liquor Control Authorities
provisions.title: Co-option of advisory personnel
provisions.content:
3A. Co-option of advisory personnel
A control authority may co-opt any person to attend any meeting of the authority on any matter for the purpose of assisting or advising the authority, but such person shall have no right to vote.
[ 28 of 2018, s. 3. ]

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment of Liquor Control Authorities
provisions.title: Secretariat
provisions.content:
3B. Secretariat
The Minister shall designate an office to be the Secretariat of a control authority.
[ 28 of 2018, s. 3. ]

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment of Liquor Control Authorities
provisions.title: Functions of Secretariat
provisions.content:
3C. Functions of Secretariat
The Secretariat shall—

definitions.definition: a person so designated under section 26
liquor control authority in the relevant district, sub district, town or city, established under section 3
a place used for the sale of intoxicating liquor, non-intoxicating drinks and snacks, for consumption on the premises
a place used for the sale of intoxicating liquor, non-intoxicating drinks in sealed vessels and snacks for consumption off the premises

quantities of goods as packaged by the manufacturer and not broken up into smaller quantities or single units, save that in the case of high value items such as fridges, furniture and bicycles, a wholesaler may sell an item in smaller quantities or single units
a place where intoxicating liquor in unsealed vessels and food can be purchased, for consumption at such place
a city or town council established under the Townships Act (Cap. 40:02) or a district council established under the Local Government (District Councils) Act (Cap. 40:01)
a place of entertainment with facilities for dancing to music or live entertainment, where intoxicating liquor is sold for consumption at such place
the treating of any person on the grounds of sex, colour, race, nationality or creed, or religion, less favourably than, or in any manner different from, any person
a person who carries on the business of negotiating contracts between parties for any product for which he is an agent for reward
obtaining a licence to enable another person, who would otherwise not qualify to obtain such a licence, to run a business
spirits, wine, beer, cider or any other fermented, distilled or spirituous liquor and includes traditional beer
a person to whom a licence has been issued under this Act

a place used for sale of—
( a ) traditional beer brewed under licence from sorghum meal and mealie-meal by fermentation of malt for commercial purposes
a licence for the operation of a liquor depot
a primary (national or local) and secondary road
a member of a control authority
a road with a road reserve of 45 metres and carriage way of seven point five metres
a road with a road reserve of 60 metres and carriage way of seven point five metres
an area made up of districts in the same geographical area
a register kept in accordance with section 9( b )
a road with a road reserve of 30 metres and carriage way of six metres
to sell by retail or wholesale and includes to barter, exchange, offer or expose for sale
cigarettes, cigarette lighters, cigarette filters, tobacco, tobacco pipes, ash trays and other related cigarette accessories
to carry on the business of selling intoxicating liquor
an authorisation permitting the holder to sell intoxicating liquor at a public place for consumption at such place, for a period not exceeding 90 days
a place used for the selling of intoxicating liquor and other beverages in bulk.
